salary_rules.important_note: Decision-support rules encoded from uploaded GOV.UK saved webpages and not legal or immigration advice.

calculation_policy.healthcare_and_education_basis: Use the basis shown in the national pay scale dataset, for example 40 hours for doctors, 37.5 hours for NHS Agenda for Change outside Scotland, 37 hours in Scotland, row-specific hours for some dentists, or FTE for teaching.

standard_skilled_worker.label: Standard Skilled Worker
standard_skilled_worker.logic: Higher of general threshold and pro-rated standard going rate.

health_and_care_worker.label: Health and Care Worker
health_and_care_worker.logic: Higher of Health and Care general threshold and pro-rated lower going rate, unless healthcare national pay scale or special salary route applies.

pre_2024_cos_extension_or_update.label: Pre 4 April 2024 certificate of sponsorship extension or update
pre_2024_cos_extension_or_update.valid_until: 2030-04-04 application deadline according to saved page wording
pre_2024_cos_extension_or_update.logic: Higher of £31,300 and pro-rated lower going rate.

immigration_salary_list_standard_route.going_rate_requirement: Use Immigration Salary List row rate and region conditions. The saved page also describes minimum salary as 80% of the route usual minimum.

health_and_care_new_entrant_or_stem_phd.note: The saved Health and Care page was not uploaded, but current GOV.UK guidance says some discounts use lower going rate with £25,000 floor. Treat as missing source unless added.

health_and_care_non_stem_phd.note: Source page should be added if the app supports Health and Care Worker route in detail.

when_can_be_paid_less.heading: When you can be paid less
content: You might still be able to apply for a Skilled Worker visa if your job is eligible but your salary is less than the standard salary requirement of £41,700 or your job’s standard ‘going rate’.
You can be paid between 70% and 90% of the standard going rate for your job if your salary is at least £33,400 per year and you meet one of the following criteria:
you’re under 26, studying or a recent graduate, or in professional training

you have a science, technology, engineering or maths (STEM) PhD level qualification that’s relevant to your job (if you have a relevant PhD level qualification in any other subject your salary must be at least £37,500)
you have a postdoctoral position in science or higher education
There are different salary rules if you work in some healthcare or education jobs or you’re extending your visa , updating your visa or switching to this visa to become a prison officer .

when_can_be_paid_less.heading: Your job is on the immigration salary list
content: The immigration salary list is a list of skilled jobs which have lower salary requirements.
If your job is on the immigration salary list you also pay a lower fee to apply for your visa.
You must be paid at least £33,400 per year if you are being sponsored for a job on the immigration salary list.
You must still be paid at least the standard going rate for your job. Check the standard going rate for your job in the going rates table .
View the immigration salary list to see if your job is included and how much you’ll need to be paid.
Make sure you check whether your job is listed in the part of the UK you’ll be working in – UK-wide, or only in England, Scotland, Wales or Northern Ireland.

when_can_be_paid_less.heading: You’re under 26, studying or a recent graduate, or in professional training
content: You can be paid 70% of your job’s standard going rate if your salary will be at least £33,400 per year and one of the following applies:
you’re under 26 on the date you apply
you’re currently in the UK on a Student visa studying at bachelor’s degree level or above (or you have been in the last 2 years, and a Student or visit visa was your most recent visa)
you’re currently in the UK on a Graduate visa (or you have been in the last 2 years, and a Graduate or visit visa was your most recent visa)
you’ll be working towards a recognised qualification in a UK regulated profession
you’ll be working towards full registration or chartered status in the job you’re being sponsored for
If this applies to you, check how much you’ll need to be paid to qualify for this visa.
Your total stay in the UK cannot be more than 4 years if you apply for one of these reasons. This includes any time you’ve already spent in the UK on a Graduate visa.

when_can_be_paid_less.heading: You have a PhD level qualification that’s relevant to your job
content: If your job is eligible for a PhD salary discount, you can be paid 80% or 90% of the job’s standard going rate, depending on which subject you are qualified in.
If you have a science, technology, engineering or maths (STEM) qualification, you can be paid 80% of your job’s standard going rate, as long as you will still be paid at least £33,400 a year.
If you have a non-STEM qualification, you can be paid 90% of your job’s standard going rate, as long as you will still be paid at least £37,500 a year.
In both situations, you must:
have a UK PhD or an equivalent doctorate-level overseas qualification - you’ll need to apply through Ecctis to check if an overseas qualification is equivalent to a UK PhD
be able to prove your qualification is relevant to the job you’ll be doing in the UK - your employer can confirm this
View the list of jobs that qualify for a PhD salary discount to see if your job is included and how much you need to be paid.
If you’re a research or academic leader, you may also be eligible to apply for the Global Talent visa . This visa has no language or minimum salary requirements.

when_can_be_paid_less.heading: You have a postdoctoral position in science or higher education
content: You can be paid 70% of your job’s standard going rate if you’ll be working in a postdoctoral position in certain science or higher education roles.
Your job must be in one of the following occupation codes to qualify for this salary discount:
2111: chemical scientists
2112: biological scientists
2113: biochemists and biomedical scientists
2114: physical scientists
2115: social and humanities scientists
2119: natural and social science professionals not elsewhere classified
2162: other researchers, unspecified discipline
2311: higher education teaching professionals
If this applies to you, check how much you’ll need to be paid to qualify for this visa.

Your total stay in the UK cannot be more than 4 years if you apply to work in a postdoctoral position at 70% of the standard going rate. This includes any time you’ve already spent in the UK on a Graduate visa.
Check if you need a UK visa
Sponsorship: guidance for employers and educators
How to apply for a visa to come to the UK

Work in the UK

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: If you got your first certificate of sponsorship before 4 April 2024
content: There are lower salary requirements if both of the following apply:
you got your certificate of sponsorship for your first Tier 2 or Skilled Worker visa before 4 April 2024
you have continually held one or more Skilled Worker visas since then
You can be paid whichever is higher of £31,300 or the lower going rate for your occupation if you make an application to:
extend your visa
update your visa

Lower salary requirements will only be in place for applications made before 4 April 2030. These salary requirements will be updated regularly and your salary will still need to rise in line with the updated going rates each time you apply.
Each occupation code has its own annual going rate. Check the lower going rate for your job in the going rates table .

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: When you can be paid less
content: You can be paid between 70% and 90% of the lower going rate for your job if your salary is at least £25,000 per year and you meet one of the following criteria:
you’re under 26, studying or a recent graduate, or in professional training

you have a science, technology, engineering or maths (STEM) PhD level qualification that’s relevant to your job (if you have a relevant PhD level qualification in any other subject your salary must be at least £28,200)
you have a postdoctoral position in science or higher education
Check how much you will need to be paid for these jobs in the going rates table .
There are different salary rules if you work in some healthcare or education jobs .

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: If your job is on the immigration salary list
content: If your job is on the immigration salary list , you need to be paid at least £25,000 or your job’s full going rate, whichever is higher.

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: If you’re under 26, studying or a recent graduate, or in professional training
content: You can be paid at least 70% of your job’s lower going rate if your salary will be at least £25,000 per year.
Your total stay in the UK cannot be more than 4 years. This includes any time you’ve already spent in the UK on a Tier 2 (General) or Graduate visa.

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: If you have a PhD level qualification that’s relevant to your job
content: If you have a science, technology, engineering or maths (STEM) PhD level qualification, you can be paid at least 80% of the lower going rate if your salary will be at least £25,000 per year.
If you have a relevant PhD level qualification in any other subject, you can be paid at least 90% of the lower going rate if your salary will be at least £28,200 per year.

pre_2024_cos_rules.heading: If you have a postdoctoral position in science or higher education
content: You can be paid at least 70% of the lower going rate if your salary will be at least £25,000 per year.
Your total stay in the UK cannot be more than 4 years. This includes any time you’ve already spent in the UK on a Tier 2 (General) or Graduate visa.

temporary_shortage_list.calculation_note: Use the rates in the Temporary Shortage List row. Rates are based on 37.5 weekly hours and should be pro-rated.

eligible_healthcare_and_education_jobs.calculation_note: If the occupation is listed and the route facts apply, calculate with healthcare or education national pay scale data rather than the ordinary going rates table.

caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.A: RQF6 salary only: £41,700 and full pro-rated going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.B: RQF6 relevant PhD: £37,500 and 90% of pro-rated going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.C: RQF6 relevant STEM PhD: £33,400 and 80% of pro-rated going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.D: RQF6 Immigration Salary List: £33,400 and full pro-rated going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.E: RQF6 new entrant: £33,400 and 70% of pro-rated going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.F: RQF3 salary only: £31,300 and full pro-rated lower going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.G: RQF3 relevant PhD: £28,200 and 90% of pro-rated lower going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.H: RQF3 relevant STEM PhD: £25,000 and 80% of pro-rated lower going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.I: RQF3 Immigration Salary List: £25,000 and full pro-rated lower going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.J: RQF3 new entrant: £25,000 and 70% of pro-rated lower going rate.
caseworker_guidance_encoded_options.K: Listed health or education occupation: £25,000 and full pro-rated national pay scale going rate.
